members.name: CanGetBuffer
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if at least [param frames] samples are available to read in the internal ring buffer.

members.name: ClearBuffer
members.summary: Clears the internal ring buffer. [b]Note:[/b] Calling this during a capture can cause the loss of samples which causes popping in the playback.

members.name: GetBuffer
members.summary: Gets the next [param frames] samples from the internal ring buffer. Returns a [PackedVector2Array] containing exactly [param frames] samples if available, or an empty [PackedVector2Array] if insufficient data was available. The samples are signed floating-point PCM between [code]-1[/code] and [code]1[/code]. You will have to scale them if you want to use them as 8 or 16-bit integer samples. ([code]v = 0x7fff * samples[0].x[/code])

members.name: GetBufferLengthFrames
members.summary: Returns the total size of the internal ring buffer in number of samples.

members.name: GetDiscardedFrames
members.summary: Returns the number of samples discarded from the audio bus due to full buffer.

members.name: GetFramesAvailable
members.summary: Returns the number of samples available to read using [method get_buffer].

members.name: GetPushedFrames
members.summary: Returns the number of samples inserted from the audio bus.

members.name: BufferLength
members.summary: Length of the internal ring buffer, in seconds. Higher values keep data around for longer, but require more memory. Value can range from 0.01 to 10. [b]Note:[/b] Setting the buffer length will have no effect if already initialized.
